oil_commodities.note: Oil elevated due to geopolitical risk (Iran tensions) and OPEC+ supply management

fed_funds_rate.dot_plot: One rate cut projected in 2026, timing uncertain
fed_funds_rate.reason: Elevated inflation, solid economic activity, geopolitical uncertainty

cpi.note: Lowest annual CPI since May 2025; food +0.4%, energy +0.6%, shelter +0.2%

non_farm_payrolls.note: First negative NFP print; unemployment steady despite job losses

analysis_summary.overall_market_brief: Mixed signals: Gold and oil rally on geopolitical risk; equities under pressure from tariff/trade war concerns and weak labor data. Fed on hold with one cut projected for 2026.
analysis_summary.gold_outlook: Bullish - trading above 200-day MA (4254), buoyed by Iran geopolitical tensions and safe-haven demand. -13.7% from March highs but recovering.
analysis_summary.sp500_outlook: Bearish near-term - below 50-day MA (6857), below 200-day MA (6621), tariff/trade war fears weighing. Support at 6500.
analysis_summary.oil_outlook: Bullish - WTI near $89, Brent at $104+. OPEC+ supply discipline + Iran geopolitical risk keep energy elevated.
key_themes: Fed holds rates at 3.5-3.75%, projects one 2026 cut
CPI cooling to 2.4% (lowest since May 2025) - disinflation trend intact
First negative NFP (-92K) signals labor market softening
Gold recovering (+1.69% today) on geopolitical safe-haven bid
Oil surges: WTI ~$89, Brent ~$104 on Iran/OPEC+ supply concerns
S&P 500 under pressure: below 50-day and 200-day MAs, trade war fears
key_risks: Trade war escalation (tariffs) could reignite inflation and derail Fed easing
Iran geopolitical situation - military conflict could spike oil above $100
Labor market deterioration beyond -92K NFP could signal recession
Gold rally at risk if dollar strengthens on safe-haven flows
S&P 500 could test 6400-6500 support if tariff tensions escalate
